I have all the source material. I have everything needed to build the inventory. Below is the complete claim inventory.

# Camera/Depth/Power Claim Inventory — cs7980-guide-mate

Reconciliation target: live hardware evidence that (a) USB2 also disconnects, and (b) under-voltage is NOT always flagged. The two flagged claims at the bottom are the ones most exposed to that evidence.

## A. USB power root cause

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| USB3 boot/disconnect loop root cause is **power, not the cable or the camera** | `docs/camera.md:15-18`, `:26-29`, `:100`, `:118`, `:136` | Settled ("✅ Root cause: power starvation"); "POWER" in heading |
| Diagnosis is power because `vcgencmd get_throttled` = `0x50000` → under-voltage **has occurred** (`0x10000`) + throttling (`0x40000`) | `docs/camera.md:122`, `:201-202` | Stated as hard evidence |
| Pi 4 caps USB at **~600 mA shared** across all ports (`max_usb_current` not set) | `docs/camera.md:123`, `:178-179` | Stated as fact |
| OAK `bMaxPower` = 500 mA (USB2), up to 900 mA on USB3; peak spin-up draw **exceeds** the Pi budget | `docs/camera.md:124` | Stated as fact |
| Link **reaches SuperSpeed every cycle** before dropping → cable data lanes are fine | `docs/camera.md:125`, `:134` | Stated as proof cable is OK |
| Streams are **flawless on USB2** → camera itself is healthy | `docs/camera.md:126`, `:133` | Stated as proof |
| Failure chain: USB3 → current spike → exceeds ~600 mA → 5 V rail browns out (under-voltage flag) → SuperSpeed collapses → re-enumerate → loop (22 cycles observed) | `docs/camera.md:115`, `:128-131` | Settled narrative |
| OAK-D-LITE is **not designed to be bus-powered off a Pi 4** at full USB3 load | `docs/camera.md:130-131` | Stated as conclusion |
| Camera **boot-loops on USB3 (power-limited)** | `CLAUDE.md:70`, `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:28-29`, `:237` | Settled (gotcha #2) |

## B. "USB2 is stable" assertion

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| Forcing USB2 (`i_usb_speed:=HIGH`) is **stable** but bandwidth-limited | `docs/camera.md:17-18`, `:25-29` | Settled ("which is stable") |
| Forcing USB2 keeps device on the **stable** high-speed link; draws/runs lighter and **stays connected**; result: 0 `X_LINK_ERROR` | `docs/camera.md:138-140` | Settled |
| Healthy USB2 start logs `USB SPEED: HIGH`, `Camera ready!`, and **no** `X_LINK_ERROR` lines | `docs/camera.md:66-68`, `:140` | Stated as the healthy/expected state |
| Both RGB + depth streams **deliver frames in USB2 mode** ("✅ live frames") | `docs/camera.md:83-88` | Verified |
| Bring it up **forced to USB2** (the standing workaround) | `CLAUDE.md:70`, `:84`, `docs/mapping/README.md:107`, `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:28-29` | Settled recommendation |

> ⚠️ **FLAG (contradiction risk):** Every "USB2 is stable / stays connected / 0 X_LINK_ERROR" statement is presented as **settled**, yet `docs/network/ros2-over-nuwave.md:86` says the OAK-D on robot 468 "**throws recurring `X_LINK_ERROR` communication failures … the device is unhealthy**" and `CLAUDE.md:76` / `docs/camera.md:144` describe an **X_LINK depth stall (0 Hz) that happens to the running (USB2) camera**. So the codebase already contains evidence that USB2 is **not** disconnect/X_LINK-error-free. If live evidence shows USB2 also disconnecting, the camera.md "stable" claim is directly contradicted.

## C. Frame-rate / bandwidth numbers

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| USB2 drops **~80 % of frames at 30 fps** | `docs/camera.md:18`, `:28`, `:164` | Stated as measured |
| Measured (20 s, both streams): RGB **5.9 fps** of 30 @ 16.4 MB/s; Depth **5.6 fps** of 30 @ 10.4 MB/s; total **~27 MB/s (214 Mbit/s)** | `docs/camera.md:149-153` | Measured |
| Both at 30 fps needs **≈135 MB/s** (RGB 81 + depth 54); USB2/XLink delivers only **~27 MB/s** | `docs/camera.md:156-158` | Stated as computed |
| Internal-consistency check: delivered/demand = 27/135 = 20% == 6/30 fps = 20% | `docs/camera.md:160-162` | Stated as self-consistent |
| Default depth bring-up = **1280×720**; mapping stack runs `align_depth:=false`+`resolution:=400` → depth is **640×480** | `docs/camera.md:90-96`, `:152` | "hardware-confirmed 2026-06-20" |
| Mapping camera runs `left/right.i_fps:=6.0`; depth ~4–22 Hz depending on fps params | `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:30-31`, `:239`, `CLAUDE.md:89` | Stated |
| Stopgaps: `fps:=5` → ~100% frames/0 drops at 5 fps; `i_low_bandwidth:=true` → RGB 2700 KB → ~50–100 KB/frame; lower resolution | `docs/camera.md:182-184` | Recommendation (low_bandwidth drop-free fps **not yet verified**, `:188`) |

## D. Pending Y-adapter / proper fix

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| Proper fix = stop powering camera from Pi USB budget → stable USB3 → full 30 fps, no drops | `docs/camera.md:18`, `:29`, `:168-171` | **Pending** |
| Power OAK independently via **Luxonis Y-adapter** (data leg→Pi, 5 V leg→separate supply) **or** a powered USB3 hub | `docs/camera.md:172-175` | Pending — "**Not yet done**" |
| Need to **confirm whether robot 468 shipped with the Y-adapter** | `docs/camera.md:175`, `:187` | Open / unverified |
| Use a proper Pi PSU (under-volt flag suggests the Pi's 5 V/3 A may be marginal; use official 15 W+) | `docs/camera.md:176-178` | Pending |
| Stopgap: add `max_usb_current=1` to `config.txt` → budget toward 1.2 A (helps, doesn't replace #1) | `docs/camera.md:178-179` | Minor/stopgap, not done |
| Re-cable with independent power and confirm stable USB3 @ 30 fps | `docs/camera.md:187` | Not yet verified (checkbox) |
| Repeat the whole camera test on **robot 436** | `docs/camera.md:189`, `:190` | Not yet checked |

## E. X_LINK stall handling & recovery procedures

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| **OAK depth stalls (X_LINK):** camera_node stays alive but stops publishing (0 Hz) | `CLAUDE.md:76`, `docs/camera.md:144` (gloss) | Stated as known behavior (gotcha #8) |
| Fusion **degrades gracefully** — passes raw lidar through on stale depth | `CLAUDE.md:41`, `:76`, `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:129-131`, `:32` | Stated as designed behavior |
| Recover with **SIGTERM-by-PID + relaunch** | `CLAUDE.md:76` | Recovery procedure (settled) |
| Depth stale threshold `max_depth_age` default **0.4 s** → raw lidar passthrough | `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:129` | Stated (config default) |
| `oakd/stop_camera` / `start_camera` services to stop/restart depth pipeline | `docs/power.md:82`, `:111`, `CLAUDE.md:73` | Verified ("Actually stops") |
| `pkill -f camera_node` matches your own shell — kill by **PID** or `ps comm`, not `-f` | `CLAUDE.md:74` | Settled gotcha (#6) |
| OAK-D on 468 "throws **recurring `X_LINK_ERROR`** … device is unhealthy" (over NUwave doc) | `docs/network/ros2-over-nuwave.md:86` | Stated as a hardware/USB issue (unrelated to networking) |
| Diagnostic to watch the loop: `dmesg \| grep -iE 'usb (1-1.2\|2-2)\|f63b\|2485\|disconnect'` | `docs/camera.md:203-204` | Diagnostic command |

## F. Resolution / camera_info mismatch

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| `oakd/stereo/camera_info` **always reports 1280×720**, but the actual mapping depth image is **640×480** → `K` doesn't match | `CLAUDE.md:75`, `docs/camera.md:90-96`, `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:30-31`, `:61` | Settled (gotcha #7) |
| Using that K unscaled **skews the projection** (whole depth wedge swings to −90°) | `CLAUDE.md:75` | Stated as observed |
| `depth_lidar_fusion` uses camera_info **only if w/h match the image**, else a **FOV pinhole model** (`hfov_deg`/`vfov_deg`) | `CLAUDE.md:75`, `docs/camera.md:95-96` | Stated as the fix in code |
| Separately, **`rplidar_link` is yawed ~90°** so the depth wedge sits near −90° (fine — lidar shares the frame) | `CLAUDE.md:75` | Stated as understood/benign |
| Measured FOV: vertical ≈ **58°** (±29°), horizontal ≈ **73°**; camera horizontal at **0.244 m**, no tilt (from TF) | `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:35-37` | "measured geometry" |
| Default vs mapping depth size and step (640×480, 16UC1, step 1280) | `docs/camera.md:94-95`, `docs/mapping/depth-perception.md:61` | "hardware-confirmed 2026-06-20" |

## G. Power-doc claims that bear on the camera/power root cause

| Claim | Source file:line | Confidence as stated |
|---|---|---|
| Robot is **bus-powered** off the Create 3 battery via USB-C (powers Create 3 **and** Pi 4) | `docs/power.md:3`, `CLAUDE.md:32` | Settled |
| Measured idle draw ~**14 W** (−1.0 A @ ~14.15 V), full stack undocked | `docs/power.md:9`, `:48`, `CLAUDE.md:73` | Measured |
| Camera is the **smallest consumer (~3% CPU)**; bus-powered so some idle USB draw remains even when stopped | `docs/power.md:82`, `:66` | Stated |
| Camera is **bus-powered with no per-port control on the Pi 4** — full power-down needs a hardware change | `docs/power.md:129` | Stated (cross-refs camera.md) |
| `oakd/stop_camera` is the **only** soft service that actually stops its pipeline | `docs/power.md:82`, `CLAUDE.md:73` | Verified |

> Note: `docs/power.md` independently confirms the bus-power architecture that the camera.md root-cause depends on (Pi + camera share the Create 3 battery rail via USB-C). This **supports** the "marginal supply" hypothesis but does not itself prove USB2-vs-USB3 is the discriminating variable.

## Claims flagged as "settled but contradictable by live evidence"

1. **"USB2 is stable / stays connected / 0 X_LINK_ERROR"** — `docs/camera.md:17-18, 26-29, 138-140, 66-68`. Presented as settled, but **already contradicted within the repo** by `docs/network/ros2-over-nuwave.md:86` (recurring `X_LINK_ERROR`, "device is unhealthy") and by the documented USB2-mode X_LINK depth stall (`CLAUDE.md:76`, gotcha #8). If live evidence shows USB2 also disconnecting/erroring, the "stable" framing is wrong; at minimum "stable" should be downgraded to "more stable than USB3, but still suffers intermittent X_LINK stalls/disconnects."

2. **"Root cause is power, proven by the under-voltage flag"** — `docs/camera.md:118-136`, esp. `:122` (`get_throttled = 0x50000`). The diagnosis leans heavily on under-voltage **having been flagged once**. If live evidence shows USB2 disconnects occur **without** an under-voltage flag (i.e., `get_throttled` clean during a disconnect), the single-cause "power starvation" conclusion is undermined — the flag is presented as necessary proof but under-voltage is not always raised on a brownout, and a USB2 disconnect with no flag would point to a data-link/firmware/XLink cause the doc explicitly rules out ("camera itself is healthy", "data lanes OK"). The `0x...0000` high-bit reading only proves under-voltage occurred **at some point since boot**, not that it co-occurred with each disconnect.

3. **Secondary/unverified-but-asserted:** `~600 mA shared USB budget` and `bMaxPower 500/900 mA exceed budget` (`docs/camera.md:123-124`) are stated as fact without an on-robot current measurement (the robot has **no per-component metering** per `docs/power.md:18`) — they are plausible spec values, not measured, so they cannot themselves confirm the brownout-at-USB3 chain.
